response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's resume demonstrates strong software engineering experience, particularly in full-stack development and AI-agent infrastructure at Google and Meta. However, his background lacks direct experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, deep-learning workload optimization, or GPU/SIMD architecture—key requirements for this Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerators. While he has contributed to high-performance systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby), the absence of domain-specific expertise in hardware optimization and AI workloads significantly reduces his fit for this position.
strengths: Extensive full-stack software engineering experience at top-tier companies (Google, Meta)
Proven track record in performance optimization for high-scale systems (e.g., micro-services, real-time data processing)
Demonstrated versatility across multiple programming languages and paradigms (C/C++, JavaScript/TypeScript, D, etc.)
concerns: No documented experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or GPU architecture
Lacks direct involvement in deep-learning workload optimization or AI accelerator design
No evidence of performance analysis for AI-specific bottlenecks (e.g., SIMD/MIMD, memory hierarchy optimization)

key_factors.factor: Direct experience with AI workload optimization
Deep technical experience in cloud infrastructure
